Name: Desire Energy Solutions Pvt. Ltd.
Description:
Desire Energy Solutions Pvt Ltd is a national award-winning tech driven water management company, headquartered in Jaipur, with business interests in energy efficiency, engineering, technology, construction and manufacturing in public water sector.
 
The Company is counted among the fastest growing water infra and management companies in India working as an aggregator with various stokeholds and having PAN India operations. Over the years, the company has successfully implemented various ESCO, solar, IoT based IT and infrastructure projects in the field of public water sector under various central and state government initiatives such as Jal Jeevan Mission, PM-Kusum, Panghat Yojna etc. The Company is currently managing water supply of more than 1,00,000+ villages and 14+ cities in India through its innovative use of technology, renewable energy and efficient management skills.
 
The Company also has a separate technology division for known as “AquaLogix” which provides advance IoT and artificial intelligence-based solutions for monitoring, predictive and preventive maintenance of water infrastructure. Through all of its services and solutions, the company aims to make public water infrastructure smart, efficient and sustainable.
 
The Company has a team of more than 2000+ professionals spread across the nation who have a proven track record and professional skills, woven together with the values of trust, transparency, partnership and technology. For its innovative and pathbreaking work, the Company has been awarded with various prestigious awards such as:
 
• National Award in service sector by Prime Minister Shree Narendra Modi (October-2016)
• Udhyog Ratna Award by Chief Minister of Rajasthan Smt. Vasundhra Raje Sindhiya (November – 2017)
• “Energy Circle Award” for innovating ESCO Model, by CII (May 2018)
• “Dare To Dream Award” by FORTI (August 2018)
•  "Asian Water Awards 2024" by Asian power

Name: NanoH2O
Description:
NanoH2O Co., Ltd. is a global water technology company delivering sustainable value across industries and communities through advanced expertise and innovation in water treatment. Originating from LG Chem’s Water Solutions business, NanoH2O has evolved into an independent company dedicated to redefining the possibilities of water technology.

The company specializes in high-performance water treatment solutions, including Reverse Osmosis (RO), Ultrafiltration (UF), and Ion Exchange (IX), serving industrial, municipal, and commercial applications worldwide. Leveraging deep technical capabilities and extensive field experience, NanoH2O addresses complex water challenges with reliable, practical, and impactful solutions.

Guided by the belief that scientific and technological innovation can improve lives, strengthen industries, and protect the planet, NanoH2O has embarked on a renewed journey of growth and transformation. With a commitment to sustainability at its core, the company continues to create meaningful impact through collaboration, pioneering technologies, and a steadfast focus on building a more sustainable future for water.

Name: Irritec
Description: Irritec Group, founded in Sicily in 1974, is one of the world leaders in smart irrigation. Irritec's mission is to improve the efficiency of irrigation in agriculture and gardening, reducing the environmental impact. Water scarcity and growing of food needs asked for the use of drip irrigation all over the world, of which Irritec is a global reference point. Irritec designs, manufactures and distributes products and complete systems for irrigation in open fields, in greenhouses and for the ladscape sector. With 14  factories and more than 700 employees, Irritec has production and sales offices in Italy, Spain, Mexico, Brazil, United States, Algeria, Germany and Chile. In its commitment to eco-sustainability, the company launched the Green Fields project, which encourages farmers to properly dispose of plastic products after use, ensuring a second life. Initiative that has made Irritec an excellence in the circular economy. Innovation and sustainability have earned numerous international awards to a company that targets growers, distributors and retailers, always aiming for maximum results while preserving the planet's most precious asset: water.

Name: WiseConn Latam
Description:
Desarrollamos soluciones de control remoto para la agricultura.
Nuestra solución DropControl es la mejor plataforma tecnológica para monitorear y controlar campos con precisión. Los datos se despliegan con un enfoque agronómico en la comodidad de la nube.
Contamos con una App Móvil y Plataforma Web que permite a los agricultores tener información en tiempo real de sus riegos, fertiirrigación, sensores climáticos, sensores de humedad del suelo, sensores de plantas y automatización de sus bombas, pozos, válvulas, reservorios y más.
Esta información le permite administrar mejor los recursos y tomar mejores decisiones en los momentos adecuados, lo que se traduce en un aumento de la productividad de los cultivos.
Contamos con más de 10.000 puntos de monitoreo y control en Chile, Perú, México, Centroamérica, Estados Unidos y Australia, con una extensa red de distribuidores certificados.
We develop remote control solutions for agriculture.
Our DropControl solution is the best technological platform to monitor and control farms with precision. The data is deployed with an agronomic approach in the comfort of the cloud.
We have a Mobile App and Web Platform that allows farmers to have real-time information on their irrigation, fertigation, climate sensors, soil moisture sensors, plant sensors and automation of their pumps, wells, valves, reservoirs and more.
This information allows you to better manage resources and make better decisions at the right times, which translates into an increase in crop productivity.
We have more than 10,000 monitoring and control points in Chile, Perú, Mexico, Central America, the United States and Australia, with an extensive network of certified distributors.

Name: Oneka Technologies
Description:
Oneka's mission is to make the oceans a sustainable and affordable source of freshwater. To that end, we offer sustainable desalination solutions that use exclusively the renewable energy from ocean waves. By using our patented desalination technology, we increase the resilience of coastal communities and industries facing water scarcity, while helping them to adapt and mitigate the impacts of climate change. 

The benefits: 
- Zero GHG emission
- Lower salinity and well diffused brine 
- Water intake safe for marine life
- No land use for desalination
- Decentralized - Grid independent
- Modular and scalable
- Potential lowest cost

Founded in 2015, Oneka now has a team of over 60 employees and operates in Canada, the United States and Chile.

Name: Pour une Hydrologie Régénérative
Description:
L’Hydrologie Régénérative est la science de la régénération du cycle de l’eau douce par l’aménagement du territoire.

    Plus spécifiquement, l’Hydrologie Régénérative se veut rassembler toutes approches visant à restaurer massivement le cycle de l’eau par l’aménagement de territoires et agroécosystèmes qui cherche à :
    – Ralentir, Répartir, Infiltrer et Stocker toutes les eaux de pluie et de ruissellement, et
    – Densifier sa végétation multifonctionnelle, cultivée ou non,
    pour améliorer leur résilience face à nombre de problématiques liées à l’eau (sécheresses, érosion, canicules, désertification, inondations, fertilité, biodiversité, évolutions climatiques,…)

Name: Rubicon Water
Description:
Rubicon Water is a publicly listed company (ASX:RWL) with a vision to sustainably increase global food and fibre production through improved water use efficiency. We do this by delivering advanced technology to managers of gravity-fed irrigation networks, enabling them to manage their water resources with unprecedented levels of efficiency and control. 

Rubicon designs, develops, manufactures and tests all its own products and proudly boasts ISO 9001:2015 certification attesting to the highest standards in the process of design and manufacturing. Our research and development facility includes one of the largest hydraulic flow and testing laboratories of its type in the Southern Hemisphere where our meters and gates are tested to international standards. A wide array of global patents over new technology built into TCC components confirms our focus upon innovation and improvement in our solutions.

Our pioneering research into the hydraulic control of channels in collaboration with the University of Melbourne has led to many important breakthroughs in flow measurement and control theory and which have been recognised by governmental and academic awards including the prestigious Australian Academy of Technological Sciences and Engineering Clunies Ross Awards. Today we jointly own the intellectual property of many patents with the University of Melbourne.

Established in 1995, Rubicon Water is headquartered in Melbourne, Australia. To support our global expansion, we have offices and manufacturing facilities in India, New Zealand, Chile, USA, Spain, China and agents in many other countries. To date, Rubicon has designed, built and installed over 35,000 control and measurement devices in TCC and FarmConnect systems sold to more than 180 customers in 17 countries. 

Website: https://www.rubiconwater.com/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/RubiconWater/

Name: Coldep
Description:
We are changing the treatment of wastewater and/or salt water. As clean-tech start-up, Coldep has industrialized patents from IFREMER and INSA Lyon in the aquaculture, industrial effluent and seawater pre-treatment sectors. 

Coldep solution is based on a game changer technology at 2 levels:

Operations: 
- Low energy consumption: (< 0.03kW.h / treated water m3) 
- No chemicals: no addition
- Water loss: less than 1% of the flow rate vs 30 at 40% in membrane-based processes 
- No consumables (vs regular washing and changing of filters, maintenance)
- Small footprint: vertical design

Outcome and added value for clients: 
- CAPEX performance: replacement of single-function process chain with a multi-function solution 
- Low OPEX: energy savings, very low maintenance, 0 consumables 
- Ease of use: reliability, resilience and simplicity of operation 
- Ecological: depollution, no chemicals, long lifespan 
- Ergonomic: ability to control the solution remotely and provide a continuous flow of information

Name: Siphofaneni Irrigation District (SID)
Description:
Siphofaneni Irrigation District (SID) is a statutory entity established in terms of the National Water Act no. 7 of 2003 and a subdivision of the National Government under MNRE, DWA (Decentralisation). SID was formed in 2007 and was originally gazetted in 2009.

Its main purposes are to control the operations and maintenance of all water works in the district and is involved in the enforcement of adherence to water regulations (Water Act and Policy). The entity is lawfully authorized to take reasonable and legal measures to avoid misuse and wastage of water. As well it levies and collect tariffs, rates as per water pricing.

Name: WiseConn
Description:
We specialize in precise irrigation solutions that drive sustainable farming practices. WiseConn empowers sustainable farming through precise irrigation solutions. 

We provide a user-friendly system for easy monitoring and control, offering farmers the most complete irrigation management system available. It is a cloud-based automation solution at the tip of your fingers.

Our comprehensive solution allows us to understand from water source analysis to agronomic implementation. We support farmers in executing efficient irrigation programs, optimizing water usage, and reducing costs such as fertilizer, energy, and labor.

Established in 2006, WiseConn has a global presence, serving regions in Latin America, the US, Europe, and Australia. With over 18 years of experience, we are committed to providing reliable, flexible, and scalable irrigation solutions that enhance resource efficiency, agriculture productivity, and long-term sustainability.

Name: OSMOSUN - Water Solutions
Description: OSMOSUN specializes in innovative water treatment solutions using renewable energy. Our reverse osmosis desalination units, OSMOSUN, make seawater (SW range) or brackish water (BW range) potable. Their manufacturing quality and energy efficiency are among the best in the market. OSMOSUN fresh water production capacity ranges from 1 m3 up to 2,500 m3 per day, and we can design facilities up to a 20,000 m3 per day capacity by combining standard units, as well as specific treatment chains for plants producing up to 50,000 m3 per day. The OSMOSUN innovation relies on its power supply. Off-grid, our units operate using photovoltaic solar energy without batteries. Functioning autonomously from sunrise to sunset, we are able to produce fresh water at a low cost in, even the most remote locations, without CO2 emissions during operation. When connected to the grid, OSMOSUN installations can produce water 24/7 through an intelligent hybridization of solar energy, the electrical grid, and/or other potential sources. The OSMOSUN process is patented and is a registered trademark.

Basically, we make it easy to spot problems before they grow and take action that actually saves water, energy, and money.

And we either help you automate it all or give your team the tools to do it themselves.

Name: Régie des Eaux de Montpellier Méditerranée Métropole
Description:
La Régie des Eaux de Montpellier Méditerranée Métropole est une entreprise publique, qui assure depuis le 1er janvier 2016, la gestion de l'eau potable et de l'eau brute. Depuis le 1er janvier 2023, la Régie assure également la gestion de l’assainissement collectif et non collectif. La Métropole de Montpellier a ainsi réaffirmé son attachement à une gestion publique de l'ensemble du petit cycle de l'eau.

La Régie, forte de ses 200 collaborateurs, s'investit au quotidien pour garantir un service public de qualité, renforcer la solidarité entre les usagers, en particulier avec la mise en œuvre de la tarification éco-solidaire et déployer un plan d'investissement massif en faveur de la préservation de la ressource en eau et de la lutte contre les pollutions dans un contexte marqué par le changement climatique.
La Régie produit et distribue 35 millions de m3 d'eau potable par an aux 414 000 habitants des 14 communes de son périmètre et investit pour l'entretien des 1350 kilomètres de réseaux et des 50 ouvrages de production et de stockage.
Concernant l'assainissement, la Régie gère la collecte et le traitement des eaux usées et son retour dans le milieu naturel sur l’ensemble du territoire métropolitain -les 31 communes et leurs 480 000 habitants- pour un volume annuel estimé à 36 millions de m3 d’eau traités. Le réseau est constitué de 1500 km de canalisations et de plus de 230 ouvrages à entretenir dont 13 stations d'épuration notamment MAERA, station pivot où sont collectées les eaux usées de 19 communes dont 14 communes de la Métropole.

Pour en savoir plus :  
https://regiedeseaux.montpellier3m.fr/
https://maera.regiedeseaux3m.fr/

Avec le changement climatique, et le stress hydrique qui en découle, nous sommes en première ligne pour préserver les ressources en eau, à chaque étape du cycle. Cette mission donne du sens à tous nos collaborateurs qui agissent au quotidien pour respecter les territoires qui nous sont confiés.

AGUR s'est positionné comme une vraie alternative dans le métier de la distribution d'eau potable. Entre la formule "grand groupe" et la formule "régie", notre modèle d'organisation allie le management collaboratif et le process au service des hommes. 
Ce système de management, qui a existé dès le début de l'aventure, favorise la responsabilisation, l'ouverture d'esprit, la solidarité, l'implication du collectif, la reconnaissance et donc l'épanouissement de chacun.
Les moyens numériques, la data, les superviseurs, les capteurs sont au service des opérateurs locaux qui ont l’autonomie de décider des meilleurs choix techniques au plus près du terrain.

Name: RDI Irrigation
Description:
RDI is a complete subsurface irrigation system with plant-responsive technology delivering water directly to the plant roots as they need.

The RDI tape, when filled with water, creates the perfect irrigation tube.
Its surface is composed by millions of micropores and its interior is infused with a hydrophilic polymer. When plants release chemical exudates as a sign of water demand, the tape reacts chemically by breaking the molecular bond and triggering the flow of water through the micropores.

RDI’ s nature driven technology uses 30 to 50% less water than Standard Subsurface Irrigation and 90% less than all others Surface Irrigation.

Produces 40% more and better yields. By responding to the plants' needs, it adapts to their absorption capacity, optimizing their health and growth.

Uses 50% less chemicals. The system's low flow provides organic activators to the rhizosphere and maintains optimum soil moisture to increase microbial activity.

RDI stays underground for years, season after season. No electricity or fuel is needed. Reduces Agriculture’s Carbon Footprint. Can be gravity fed and works in harsh climates & non-arable land. Enables green spaces in urban areas using reclaimed and treated greywater. Works on rooftops, vertical walls and city parks decreasing urban heat island effect.

LANDSCAPE - LAWN - SPORTS FIELDS - HOME GARDENS - SMALL TO LARGE FARMS - GREENHOUSES - VINEYARDS - ORCHARDS

Name: Eastern Irrigation District
Description:
The Eastern Irrigation District (EID), one of eleven irrigation districts in southern Alberta operates under the authority of the Alberta Irrigation Districts Act. The administrative boundaries of the EID, bounded by the Bow River in the south and the Red Deer River in the north, encompass approximately 1.5 million acres of land.

The District’s primary business is the management of an extensive raw water distribution network in support of irrigated agriculture. In addition to conveying raw water to over 315,000 acres of irrigated farmland owned by private families and corporations, the EID also conveys raw water through their distribution network to benefit municipal, industrial, wildlife habitat and recreational purposes. The EID also owns an expanse of native and improved grassland, managed for the benefit of the irrigators and primarily supporting the local cattle industry.

The Eastern Irrigation District is governed by a Board of seven Directors elected by and from the 900 irrigators of the District. The EID employs approximately 75 individuals who carry out the day to day management and operation of the District.

Orteam, sadece sulama sistemleri değil, aynı zamanda tarımda verimliliği artıran, su kaynaklarını koruyan ve sürdürülebilir bir gelecek inşa eden bir ortaklıktır.

Name: Bayou Meto Water Management District
Description:
Irrigated, row-crop farming of soybeans, rice, cotton, corn, and sorghum is the primary driver of the economy of the Mississippi Alluvial Plain of East Arkansas. Irrigation water availability and good drainage are essential. 

About 85 percent of the water used for irrigation in Arkansas comes from the ground. Although Arkansas has abundant surface water, groundwater has been the preferred source. Not all farmland has access to surface water and moving it long distances is often more expensive than pumping from shallow wells. Beginning around 1920, rice farmers in Arkansas County noted that the level of the shallow and prolific alluvial aquifer declined from year-to-year. The United States Geological Survey began studying groundwater in the state and continues an extensive monitoring program one hundred years later. 

The Bayou Meto Water Management Project was conceived in the 1930s as a way to improve drainage in sluggish Delta streams, thus reducing flood damage and to put a fraction of the volume of the Arkansas River to work to irrigate nearby farms. In 1950, the Congress authorized the project, but did not provide money for construction. Continuing resource problems reinvigorated interest, and the project was reauthorized in 1996. Planning and design culminated in construction of the Marion Berry Pump Station at Scott, Arkansas, and the Little Bayou Meto Pump Station at Reydell, Arkansas, 49 straight-line miles from the Marion Berry Pump Station. Both stations were completed by 2015, and construction continues on the network of canals and pipelines to distribute the water to approximately 268,000 square miles of farmland. Water will not be pumped for several more years. 

The Little Bayou Meto Pump Station will remove water from the lower project area, including the 33,000-acre Bayou Meto Wildlife Management Area, the premier winter Mallard habitat, which attracts thousands of hunters every winter. Preventing retention

Name: AL/MS Section of AWWA
Description:
What is AWWA?
The American Water Works Association (AWWA) is an international nonprofit scientific and educational society dedicated to the improvement of drinking water quality and supply.

Founded in 1881, AWWA is the largest organization of water supply professionals in the world. Its more than 50,000 members represent the full spectrum of the drinking water community: treatment plant operators and managers, scientists, environmentalists, manufactures, academicians, regulators, and others who hold genuine interest in water supply and public health.

Membership includes more than 4,000 utilities that supply water to roughly 180 million people in North America.

Our Mission
The American Water Works Association is dedicated to the promotion of public health and welfare in the provision of drinking water of unquestionable quality and sufficient quantity. AWWA must be proactive and effective in advancing the technology, science, management, and government policies relative to the stewardship of water.

Our Vision
AWWA will be the leading force in the world dedicated to safe drinking water.

Name: Water Resources North
Description:
Water Resources North (WReN) is a collaborative group dedicated to long-term water resources planning across Yorkshire and the North East of England. Our core members—Northumbrian Water, Yorkshire Water, and Hartlepool Water—are joined by a wide range of stakeholders who share an interest in securing the region’s water future.

We work closely with:
- our customers,
- water-dependent industries such as agriculture and energy, and
- key stakeholders including environmental groups and regulators, to co-create a resilient, sustainable plan for managing water resources.

We face a significant challenge: delivering on the ambitions of the National Framework for Water Resources. That means we must:

- increase drought resilience,
- improve environmental sustainability,
- reduce long-term water use and leakage,
- minimise reliance on drought permits and orders, and
- boost water supply capacity.


Together, we’re shaping a future where water is managed responsibly, equitably, and sustainably for generations to come.

Name: Global Water Farms Inc.
Description:
In a world that is water challenged, Global Water Farms (GWF) sustainably manufactures 'new' freshwater with renewable energy. We use geothermal energy to desalinate unusable salty water; without boiling, without membranes and with Zero Liquid Discharge. In other words, inputs: salt water, sunshine – outputs: distilled water and dry salt. Inert dry salt is our only waste stream and we monetize it by manufacturing encased salt-based construction blocks to replace cement cinderblocks and the carbon emissions they produce.

Global Water Farms is a green deep tech start up in La Quinta, California that's just weeks away from constructing a full-scale demonstration Pilot on the east side of the Salton Sea. The demonstration Pilot will confirm our projected volumes of distilled water and salt production as well as how much electricity our water farms generate. This is the last step before we go fully commercial and start providing 'new' water in southern California.

See our white paper about sustainable inland desalination as a practical and commercially viable way to supply LA and other coastal cities with a constant supply of freshwater made in the desert. This approach also solves the environmental disaster at the Salton Sea. Join us on our journey to heal the planet with new water.

Name: unitec Energietechnik GmbH
Description:
COMPANY PROFILE
Unitec Energietechnik GmbH produces hot and cold water tanks made of steel and stainless steel, individually and standardized in sizes from 200 to 200,000 liters.
Thanks to many years of know-how, tanks for every need can be individually planned and delivered in the shortest possible time. Even small openings in single or multi-family houses, commercial or industrial buildings put the flexibility of unitec Energietechnik GmbH no limits - spot welds on site are one of our strengths.

OVER 150 DIFFERENT NORM PRODUCTS STORED PERMANENTLY
From buffer and combined storage tanks to stainless steel and enamel boilers to fresh water modules, heat recovery and charging systems - the permanent storage of all types of tanks including equipment ensures extremely short delivery times.

OPTIMIZED TANK TECHNOLOGY
Alternative energies have become an integral part of commodity scarcity and inflation. Solar systems, modern automatic pellet heating or heat pumps are the heating technology of today and tomorrow. The systems are powerful, environmentally friendly, CO² neutral and domestic, renewable energy sources are used.
In order to use the system technology particularly efficiently, an intelligent tank technology is needed that optimizes the heating system and the hot water preparation. Between the heat recovery - like as solar (during the hours of sunshine) and heat (heating, hot water) - is often a period of time that can be optimally bridged by the use of energy storage. By using an optimized tank, the energy efficiency or operating load (cycle times) can be improved.

INDIVIDUAL TANK TECHNOLOGY
Business and industry require very different tank solutions than single or multi-family houses. We configure the tailor-made solution for every initial situation - with perfectly coordinated components and well thought-out design.

3-Water resource engineering; including stormwater management, sustainably built environment, water resource modeling, and watershed management.
4-Hydrological modeling; encompasses surface and subsurface hydrology, flood risk, water sustainability, hydrogeology (groundwater)
5-GIS, Remote sensing, and google earth engine.
6-Wastewater; water/wastewater treatment, and biochemical engineering are the main scopes.
7-Dam design and its related infrastructure; including dam structural design, geotechnical engineering, and dam site investigation.
8-River; we seek river rehabilitation and restoration project, river dynamic, and investigation of river water quality and pollution.

Naturally as a society we have increased in numbers, mostly living in densely populated areas. As a direct result mains water quality has decreased. This has in turn opened up a market for trusted water treatment equipment and knowledge of local clean water sources.

The main reasons people have accepted using tap or high street bottled water is down to both lack of time and lack of understanding.

For these reasons we have come up with various different solutions that will enable people to have better water in their homes and places of work. 

If you are interested in hearing more about how we can help please contact us directly and speak to one of our team...

In a region of around 9,000 square kilometres, around 2.5 million people receive Fernwasser water every day. Every year the company provides more than 80 million cubic metres of drinking water from the surface water of the Rappbode dam system in the Harz Mountains, the bank filtrate water of the Elbe and the groundwater of the Dübener and Dahlener Heide. In the waterworks Wienrode (near Blankenburg in the Harz Mountains), Torgau-Ost and Mockritz in the Elbaue, up to 340,000 cubic metres of drinking water can be treated per day, which is distributed through our more than 800 kilometres long pipeline network in Central Germany. Our roster of manufacturers includes industry leaders such as Blue-White Industries, Grundfos, Lakos, Kuriyama, Kobold, and LaMotte Company, ensuring that our clients have access to cutting-edge technologies for chemical feed systems, filtration solutions, disinfection methods, and more. These partnerships allow us to deliver high-quality, reliable solutions that meet the diverse needs of water treatment facilities across multiple industries, from wastewater management to drinking water systems.

At Mullarkey Associates, we understand the regulatory challenges our clients face and are committed to providing customized solutions that prioritize safety, efficiency, and environmental responsibility. Whether you require chemical dosing systems, filtration units, or advanced water quality monitoring tools, our team of experts is dedicated to helping you achieve your goals with tailored solutions and exceptional service.

Our dedication to customer success goes beyond delivering products. Name: St. Mary River Irrigation District
Description:
We, the staff and Board at the St. Mary River Irrigation District (SMRID), are fiercely proud that we support so much of the economic, agricultural, and environmental activities in Southern Alberta.

We are dedicated to our vision of supporting communities, environment, and agriculture in the area and to our mission to provide for future generations.

We strive to be committed citizens in Southern Alberta and are proud of the place we have in the history of irrigation and its positive impacts on the lives of citizens and our place in the past, present, and future of the area.

In order to maintain the delivery of water through a complex system of reservoirs, canals and pipelines to the many water users who are within our District, we have a staff of over 100 comprising full-time and part-time field and office staff in Lethbridge, Taber and Bow Island. These staff are responsible for the engineering, operations and administration of the District.

We have eight licenses to divert water from the St. Mary, Waterton and Belly rivers. The volume of our allowable diversion is 880,000 ac/ft; on average we divert around 60% of our total license.

Our licenses not only allow water for irrigation but for other uses such as municipal, industrial, recreational and environmental; it must be noted though that we will not allow water to be used for well injection.

Due to the investment of millions of dollars by the District and irrigators to improve the efficiency of transport and application of water to crops, the District has seen water volumes reduce while irrigated acreage increase.

Name: ToDrinQ EU
Description:
ToDrinQ is a EU-funded project aimed at developing and testing a compendium of modular, complementary, innovative solutions that provide new information and better support tools to operators and designers to adapt to changes in water quality, while obtaining high drinking water quality at the tap.

ToDrinQ develops novel real-time sensing and water quality monitoring technologies, innovative treatment systems (especially suitable for small-scale/modular, adaptable treatment plants) and interoperable decision tools that support resilient, evidence-based treatment plant design and improved overall water system operational awareness and response. 

The consortium is perfectly placed to achieve significant progress beyond the state of art, based on a research-technology alliance of leading universities and research institutes and innovative technology developers including deep-tech SMEs.

It is also ideally placed to maximise relevance and impact by grounding its innovations on diverse real-world cases through co-creation with five pro-active water companies (in the Netherlands, Greece, France, Switzerland, and the Czech Republic), and maximise outreach through the influential multi-stakeholder, network Water Europe.

Membre de l'Union des Industriels et des entreprises de l'Eau (UIE), le syndicat participe à la performance énergétique, sanitaire et environnementale des réseaux d'eau dans les installations industrielles, tertiaires et immobilières.

•	représenter ses adhérents auprès des organismes nationaux, européens et internationaux

The Group operates as a producer and distributor of materials for irrigation systems, basing its activity on the know-how of its employees and offering to the customer the expertise of the companies.

They engage in synergistic work optimizing strategic and operational processes in order to create distinctive value for customers in agricultural, landscape and sports sectors.
Two values guide the activity of the Group:
The companies share a customer centric philosophy that focuses on customer satisfaction and its needs, are constantly looking for innovative solutions that aim for sustainability in terms of water saving.

Name: Ministry of Water Development and Sanitation
Description:
The Ministry of Water Development and Sanitation is responsible for developing and managing water resources, providing clean water supply, and adequate sanitation for all. Water is a finite resource like any other natural resource but is critical for the country's sustainable development. The mandate of the Ministry is to provide policy guidance in the water sector.

WATER SECTOR OVERVIEW
1991 saw a change in the Government of the Republic of Zambia that introduced general public service reforms and the liberalisation of the economy. This created a conducive environment for water sector reforms. In order to implement the reforms, a guide was required for the entire process. Thus, seven sector principles outlined in the 1994 National Water Policy were adopted as a first step to the implementation of the water sector reforms

Name: BORDA South Africa
Description:
BORDA (Bremen Overseas Research and Development Association) is a civil society expert organization founded in 1977 by representatives of the public sector, the business community, civil society and academia with aim to strengthen development cooperation on municipality level. In Africa, BORDA operates in Tanzania, Mali, Zambia and South Africa.
BORDA has been working in South Africa since 2006 to extend the waste infrastructure for inhabitants of peri-urban areas. we have projects in new and existing low-income housing developments, informal settlements and schools.
 
In South Africa, BORDA's role is to market, network, research, educate and advise on Decentralized Wastewater treatment. Collaboration with multiple local partners is a key ingredients, enabling us to carry out our activities as effectively as possible in the local context. Join us in making a difference, one project at a time.

Name: An Dour - Service Public de l'Eau
Description:
Créée le 26 juin 2023 et opérationnelle au 1er janvier 2024, An Dour est la régie de service public de l’eau des 26 communes de Morlaix Communauté. 

Elle est chargée de la mise en œuvre de la politique intercommunale de l’eau. Un choix de territoire fort dans un contexte d’enjeu de société et d’avenir de cette ressource essentielle. 

La particularité de notre régie réside dans l’approche globale et intégrée de la gestion de l’eau, de la source à la mer (petit et grand cycle de l’eau). Une première en France.

3 défis majeurs nous attendent :
-	Couvrir durablement les besoins en eau face à la baisse de la ressource 
-	Améliorer le traitement des eaux usées pour préserver les milieux et la biodiversité
-	Gérer écologiquement nos masses d’eau pour assurer tous les jours une eau potable de qualité.

Name: iWater Management
Description:
iWater Management: Revolutionizing Water Solutions with Innovation and Integrity

Water is an essential resource, yet it’s often taken for granted. At iWater Management, we are at the forefront of delivering sustainable, efficient, and innovative water management solutions. With a commitment to excellence and cutting-edge technology, we understand the intricate needs of various industries and communities, positioning ourselves as a leading provider in water treatment and management services. Our journey is driven by passion, innovation, and an unwavering dedication to making a meaningful impact.

We specialize in comprehensive water treatment solutions, including borehole water treatment, rain harvesting, wastewater recycling, and municipal water backup systems. Our expertise extends to designing and implementing efficient water storage tank systems and eco-friendly water purification processes.

At #iwatermanage, our mission is to deliver high-standard products and services through meticulously designed water treatment processes. Our services encompass:

- Borehole Drilling
- Filtration Systems
- Wastewater Recycling
- Piping and Pump Installation
- Solar-Powered Water Treatment Plants
- Dam and Reservoir Construction
- Water Storage Tanks

By leveraging internationally proven technologies, we remove and convert contaminants from various water sources—boreholes, municipal supplies, rivers, seas, rainwater, and wastewater—using eco-friendly chemicals and advanced filtration processes. We are committed to recycling, reusing, and treating all water types with the utmost care, ensuring that every drop is safe for commercial, industrial, or potable use.

Whether for human or animal consumption, or for industrial discharge, we make water safe, clean, and sustainable. Join us at iWater Management in our mission to preserve and optimize one of the world’s most vital resources.

Name: Sustainable Food & Water
Description:
Sustainable Food & Water Pty Ltd is the licensed distributor of Watergen's Atmospheric Water Generation technology and Vertical Field's agriponic technology.

Our Watergen products range from units delivering 30L of fresh drinking water per day right through to 6,000L per day and make water from thin air in a wide range of applications including mining, indigenous communities and schools.

Our Vertical Field technology allows us to develop vertical farming solutions that grow crops close to market. This means fresher crops, longer shelf life, reduced wastage, reduced transport costs and emissions and the ability to grow any crop in any season. This technology also eliminates the weather cycle risk which is the largest uncontrollable variable in traditional farming.

Our vision is to provide Australian & South Pacific households, businesses and communities with sustainable food & water solutions whilst meeting as many of the UN’s 17 Sustainable Development Goals as possible.

Our mission is to deliver sustainable food & water solutions by providing world-proven technology products by partnering with leaders in relevant market segments.

Vanuit passie voor duurzaamheid, is HWT 6 jaar geleden begonnen met het uitbreiden van zijn passie voor water. Hiervoor is er al ruim 25 jaar aan kennis binnen de hoveniers branche opgedaan. We werken zowel voor de korte, als de lange termijn aspecten. Zo willen we voor de kinderen van onze kinderen een gezonde en veilige toekomst creëren.
Met een team van 25 gespecialiseerde hoveniers zorgen we voor draagkracht en kunde, voor aanvragen van groot tot klein formaat. Il comprensorio è stato poi ampliato con due aree aggiuntive: in Piemonte, l’alta pianura novarese e in Lombardia la pianura dell’oltrepò pavese.

In totale, il comprensorio si estende ora su 5 provincie e 254 comuni per una superficie di 334.505 ettari, di cui il 44,6 % in Piemonte e il 55,4% in Lombardia, confermando così il suo carattere interregionale.

Name: IMA WATER
Description:
Somos una empresa especializada en depuración y potabilización de agua industrial y urbana con mas de 16 años de  experiencia en el campo del tratamiento de agua, con sedes de Alicante, Valencia y Murcia. 
Nuestro objetivo principal es conseguir dar un paso adelante en los problemas futuros de tratamiento de agua en todo el mundo.
Por ello dia tras dia luchamos por desarrollar nuevas formas y productos para agua potable y residual.
Nuestro trabajo para el cliente no se resume a realizar o suministrar las instalaciones. En IMA WATER nos enorgullecemos de prestar un servicio completo y profesional que comprende los siguientes puntos de acción:

- Visita al cliente y análisis del agua. Nos acercamos a los problemas y necesidades del cliente con el fin de darle la mejor solución.
- Diseño y fabricación de la planta teniendo en cuenta las necesidades actuales y futuras del cliente (Realizamos dos tipos de instalaciones: modulares o en campo, según características del entorno).
- Instalación, puesta en marcha y seguimiento de la planta durante el tiempo que sea necesario hasta que funcione a régimen.

Algunas de nuestras instalaciones más comunes son:
- Plantas pobilizadoras para ríos y lagos.
-Tecnologías de eliminación especifica de contaminantes del agua (arsénico, metales, fluoruros..)
- Desalinizadoras para agua de pozo y salina mediante ósmosis inversa.
- Instalaciones de tratamiento de agua industrial.

- Reverse osmosis and nanofiltration systems

- Deionization and demineralization
- UV disinfection and ozone treatment
- Water softening and filtration
- Process water treatment and recycling
- High-purity water for pharmaceutical and laboratory use

Why Choose WET:
As part of the MCI family of companies, we combine decades of water treatment expertise with world-class manufacturing capabilities from our Dallas, Texas facility. This provides superior quality, competitive pricing, and responsive customer service.

Our Quality Definition:
"Meeting our customers' requirements, every requirement, every time."

Name: Inflotec GmbH
Description:
Die Inflotec GmbH ist eine Ausgründung der Hochschule Magdeburg-Stendal und wurde am 04.02.2020 gegründet. Das Unternehmen hat seine Wurzeln in der experimentellen Forschung und Entwicklung von Systemen zur Wasseraufbereitung an der Hochschule Magdeburg-Stendal, Fachbereich Ingenieurwissenschaften und Industriedesign (2013-2019). 
Die Kernkompetenz der Inflotec GmbH liegt in der Entwicklung und dem Bau von mobilen, autarken Trinkwasseraufbereitungsanlagen, die ressourcenschonend verschmutztes Oberflächenwasser zu Trinkwasser aber auch Brauchwasser aufbereiten. Der innovative Ansatz der Systeme liegt in der autonomen und effizienten Funktionsweise und einer Vielzahl weiterer Alleinstellungsmerkmale. 

So wurden bereits mehrere autonome Systeme zur Wasseraufbereitung aber auch -beförderung entwickelt und zum Teil patentiert. Das System Waver verfügt über eine Patentierung im Bereich „Vorrichtung zur Wasseraufbereitung“ (Patent: 10 2015 013 916) und beschreibt das Verfahren und die Funktionsweise der Aufbereitungsanlage.

Name: Dropinsea
Description:
Dropinsea is a social enterprise dedicated to providing long-term water access solutions for rural communities in South Africa. We focus on building water independence, empowering communities to manage their own water security.

Our journey began in 2017 with a WASH (Water, Sanitation & Hygiene) program specifically targeting the needs of vulnerable women and children in Boschfontein, a village in the Nkomazi region. We quickly recognized the need for a more sustainable approach.


Our current model tackles water insecurity at its source. We partner with local drilling firms to repair existing boreholes, a critical water source for Boschfontein. But it doesn't stop there. During these repairs, we ensure skilled community members receive comprehensive training on borehole maintenance. This empowers the community to address future issues themselves, fostering self-reliance and long-term water security.

Join us on our mission!

Dropinsea is committed to making a lasting impact on rural communities in South Africa. Follow our journey as we empower Boschfontein and expand our reach to other water-insecure villages.

Name: HANYCO (Hamoun Nyzeh Co.)
Description:
HANYCO (Hamoun Nyzeh Co.) manufacturer of Ductile Iron Pipes & Fittings was established in May 2006,  in center of Iran, Kashan city, Isfahan Province, with the aim of producing Ductile Iron Pipes & Fittings in the size range DN 80 to DN 1200 in order to supply water and for sewage projects, with annual capacity of 70,000 metric tons. Installation activities of this complex were started by the private sector capital and from December 2007 mass production was started. In the recent years HANYCO`s capacity increased to 150.000 metric tones annually. 

HANYCO'S pipes & fittings are manufactured and supplied by the most modern and updated  technology according to the last version of international standards ISO 2531 and EN 545. Almost the joints are based on TYTON, but we can supply Flange pipes in several length according to customer order. The products are coated by Bituminous or Epoxy painting according to ISO 8971-2 and lined with cement mortar lining according ISO 4179. For sewage usages also appropriate linings according to EN 598 will be supplied. The Ductile Iron fittings will be coated by Fusion Bond Epoxy. We also supply the appropriate EPDM gaskets according to ISO 8643 for our pipes. 

HANYCO has a complete range of Management Standards, including ISO 9001:2008 , ISO 14001: 2009, OHSAS 18001: 2007, ISO 10002:2005, ISO 10004:2010 from DQS - Germany. 

HANYCO has operated the most modern Lost Foam casting line for producing a diverse range of Ductile Iron Fittings according to International Standards and customer request. We can also produce a wide range of ductile iron and gray cast iron parts for other industries such as automotive, agricultural machines, heavy machines and so on.

For further information, you can visit our English website: http://en.hanyco.ir/. Or contact our sales department on: sales@hanyco.ir and also  vaziri@hanyco.eu if you are contacting from Europe and North Africa.

Name: O'Mad
Description:
O'Mad, l’innovation au service de l’eau

O'Mad est une entreprise jeune et ambitieuse, spécialisée dans la distribution des solutions LM Innovation, visant à structurer et optimiser la qualité ainsi que l’efficacité de l’eau. Depuis sa création en juin 2024, cette société, basée à Bruz près de Rennes, connaît une forte croissance et s’adresse à une clientèle diversifiée, composée aussi bien de particuliers que de professionnels, dans différents secteurs : culture, irrigation, industrie, collectivités, piscines, élevage, hôtellerie-restauration, etc.

Dirigée par trois associés – Pierre Suhard, Samuel Hays et Laurent Cadorel – O'Mad s’inscrit dans une démarche environnementale en proposant des technologies qui allient performance économique et respect de l’environnement. En distribuant ces technologies, l’entreprise aide ses clients à réduire leur utilisation de produits chimiques tout en améliorant la structure de l’eau. Ces solutions offrent également d’autres avantages, tels que la restitution des propriétés naturelles de l’eau, pour un usage plus sain et plus respectueux de l’écosystème.

Grâce à sa présence active sur l’ensemble du territoire du Grand Ouest, O'Mad se positionne comme un acteur engagé dans la transition écologique et dans l’accompagnement des particuliers et des professionnels, en répondant aux besoins croissants d’alternatives durables pour la gestion et la structuration de l’eau. Il est essentiel de redonner à l’eau toutes ses propriétés naturelles.

 Pour en savoir plus sur nos technologies et découvrir comment elles peuvent répondre à vos besoins, notre équipe se tient à votre disposition pour échanger avec vous.

Name: LW: Zweckverband Landeswasserversorgung
Description:
Leben ohne Trinkwasser? Undenkbar! 
Die Landeswasserversorgung ist ein kommunaler Zweckverband und zählt zu den größten Fernwasserversorgern in Deutschland. Wir sind verantwortlich für 3 Millionen von Menschen in Baden-Württemberg und in einigen Gemeinden Bayerns. Trinkwasser ist die Grundlage unseres Lebens und das wertvollste Gut, das wir haben. Sicherzustellen, dass jederzeit und für alle Menschen Wasser in bester Qualität zur Verfügung steht, ist unsere Mission. Wir investieren daher bedarfsgerecht und vorausschauend in die Zukunft, z.B. durch die Sicherung und Erschließung von Wasserressourcen, die Modernisierung unseres Leitungsnetzes und zugehöriger Anlagen und die angestrebte CO₂-Neutralität, etwa durch den Bau von Windkraft- und Photovoltaikanlagen. Diese lebenswichtige Aufgabe zu meistern erfordert viele Talente aus unterschiedlichen Disziplinen. 

Wir garantieren Qualität und Sicherheit
Bestes Trinkwasser und eine hohe Versorgungssicherheit stehen im Mittelpunkt unseres Handelns.

Wir gestalten die Zukunft
Wir gehen sorgsam mit den uns anvertrauten Wasservorkommen und Betriebsanlagen um. Die Mitarbeiter sind dabei unser wichtigstes Kapital.

Wir sind innovativ
Wir gestalten die Zukunft durch eine ständige Verbesserung unserer Arbeitsabläufe und die Weiterentwicklung unseres Wissens.

Wir sind die Wasserexperten
Wir arbeiten für unsere Kunden mit höchster fachlicher Kompetenz.

Wir sind ein Team
Umsichtig, partnerschaftlich und leistungsorientiert erzielen wir herausragende Ergebnisse.

Name: EcoRural Project
Description:
The project aims to understand and address the multifaceted challenges hindering the sustainability of rural water systems. It employs an interdisciplinary approach to gather insights from local communities, focusing on their experiences, needs, and the underlying causes of system failures. By documenting these challenges and promoting peer-to-peer learning, the project seeks to develop tailored capacity-building activities and introduce practical solutions that address technical, social, and environmental factors. This inclusive approach aims to enhance the sustainability of rural water systems and improve access to safe drinking water for dependent communities.

Project Mission: To promote the ecological sustainability of rural water systems by developing inclusive management capacities and ensuring access to safe drinking water in rural communities.

Project Vision: A world where all rural communities have access to clean, safe, and affordable water, managed sustainably for generations to come.

Project Values: Community empowerment, inclusivity, sustainability, innovation, and collaboration.

Location: Kenya and Ghana

Partner Organizations : Strathmore University, Siaya-Bondo Water and Sanitation Company Ltd, Sirembe Community Water and Sanitation Project, African Water Watch Limited, Community Water and Sanitation Agency, Kumasi Technical University, IHE Delft, DAME-DAME DEVELOPMENT ALTERNATIVES LIMITED

Name: MED-WET project
Description:
This project, Improving MEDiterranean irrigation and Water supply for smallholder farmers by providing Efficient, low-cost and nature-based Technologies and practices (MED-WET), provides solutions to enhance irrigation efficiency as well as to increase freshwater availability by tapping into non-conventional water sources.  

During this project, three low-cost, lean solutions that optimise natural resource use and income even at small scales shall be explored. The Self-regulating, Low Energy, Clay based Irrigation (SLECI) technology is a self-regulating subsurface irrigation technique that uses the actual suction force of the surrounding soil for regulation of the system’s water release via clay tubes. The second technology is a simple solar desalination system that will be used on saline and low-grade water to recuperate freshwater suitable for irrigation. Lastly, productive constructed wetlands will be used for wastewater reuse and its transformation into reclaimed irrigation water. The technologies will be implemented in pilot sites in Portugal, Malta, Morocco, and Egypt, and will be used for the irrigation of crops, vines, as well as date, citrus and olive trees. 

MED-WET has established a consortium of partners with the competence and commitment to meet the requirements of this call. The lead partner from Germany (Hochschule Wismar), will be joined by partners from Egypt (Heliopolis University for Sustainable Development), Morocco (Institut National de la Recherche Agronomique du Maroc and Sultan Moulay Slimane University), Portugal (University of Beira Interior and Municipality of Fundão) and Malta (MCAST and the EcoGozo Directorate within the Ministry for Gozo) during this 3-year project (2021 - 2024).

MED-WET is 100% funded by the PRIMA initiative of Member States, Associated Countries and Participating Countries. The PRIMA Programme is supported by the European Union.

Name: GreenJet

Dank der TÜV-zertifizierten Formel1-Technik von GREENJET wird ab dem Einbau eine Ersparnis von bis zu 66% erzielt!

Die komplexe Form des Innenteils erzeugt dabei einen starken Unterdruck, der zu einer hohen Sogwirkung führt. Dem so genannten Venturieffekt, der eben beispielsweise in der Formel 1 genutzt wird, um Abtrieb zu erzeugen. 

Durch die 3 Bohrungen an der Außenseite wird so Luft angesaugt und in eine Kammer im Innenteil geführt. In der Kammer wird die Luft anschließend durch 6 Bohrungen dem Wasser kreisförmig zugeführt. Somit wird der Wasserstrahl optimal mit Luft ummantelt.

Name: Lesotho Botswana Water Transfer Project

The Project will transfer 150 million m3 of water annually to Botswana, while supplying various users in Lesotho and South Africa, with 50 million m3. The feasibility studies are exploring a large dam that will also generate electricity, supply Bloemfontein and enable irrigation for Lesotho.

Name: Sydney Desalination Plant Pty Limited
Description:
The Sydney Desalination Plant is an essential component of Sydney’s water supply resilience supplying up to 250 megalitres per day of drinking water (15% of Sydney’s water needs). 
The plant was built between 2008 and 2010 in response to the millennium drought and operated successfully for two years.
With water storage dams recovering to high levels, a decision was made to place the plant in a state of long term preservation ready to respond to the next drought.
In late 2015 a tornado hit the plant causing extensive damage, and requiring thorough inspection and reinstatement to its pre-storm condition. This was completed successfully in late 2018.
In January 2019, after seven years of preservation, the plant was successfully restarted and continues to be available to assist the supply of water to Sydney’s customers.

Originally the industrial business unit of De Watergroep - Flanders’ largest drinking water company - Azulatis now operates as an independent company backed by two strong shareholders: De Watergroep and TINC, a publicly listed investment firm. Description:
Yaku es una startup que ha desarrollado un innovador biofiltro para el tratamiento sustentable de las aguas grises. La solución biológica que desarrollamos es una planta de tratamiento de aguas residuales de tamaño reducido con tecnología de biofiltros para: Viviendas unifamiliares (para 500, 1.000 y 2.000 litros de agua gris); Empresas e industrias interesadas en reutilizar agua en sus procesos (desde 5.000 litros de agua gris) ; Inmobiliarias y constructoras interesadas en incorporar reutilización de agua en nuevos proyectos. 
Las aguas aguas grises corresponden al agua que proviene del uso doméstico, como el lavado de ropa y ducha. Las aguas grises pueden ser utilizadas en campos de regadío, especialmente en aquellas localidades afectas por sequía o desabastecimiento del recurso.

La tecnología que desarrollamos en nuestras estaciones depuradoras de aguas residuales para el tratamiento de las aguas grises, permite darle un segundo uso al agua y redestinarla a procesos que no requieren agua potable tales como: agua para el excusado, riego y usos industriales

Nuestro biofiltro tiene la capacidad para autoregenerarse una vez instalado. Requiere un mínimo control y baja mantención y es la óptima solución para áreas urbanas y rurales que necesitan optimizar el uso del agua y del espacio. Nuestro biofiltro se sumerge dentro del estanque de aguas grises, optimizando en espacio necesario y permitiendo su funcionamiento eficiente.

Yaku es una Startup constituida por científicos especialistas en el área microbiología, capaces de diseñar la óptima solución para el tratamiento y reutilización de aguas residuales del proceso industrial de tu empresa o faena. Con nosotros podrás dar cumplimiento a los estándares exigidos por la Ley 21.075 que “Regula la recolección, reutilización y disposición de aguas grises”.

Contáctanos para evaluar la solución adecuada para tu hogar o empresa, así podrás das cumplimiento a la ley junto con contribuir al cuidado del agua.

La società assolve principalmente i compiti della gestione, esercizio e manutenzione di grandi opere idrauliche ed agisce quale fornitore all’ingrosso di acqua non trattata, per usi potabili agli acquedotti Pugliese, Lucano ed al Consorzio Jonio Cosentino in Calabria; per usi irrigui a nove consorzi di bonifica nelle regioni Basilicata, Campania e Puglia, e per usi industriali all’ILVA di Taranto e ad altri utenti minori.

Vengono, inoltre, sfruttati alcuni salti idraulici per la produzione di energia elettrica che viene immessa nella rete nazionale. L’attività della società si esplica attraverso la gestione di otto dighe, di quattro traverse, delle sorgenti del Tara e di centinaia di chilometri di grandi reti di adduzione, con una capacita potenziale di accumulo, regolazione e di vettoriamento di circa un miliardo di metri cubi l’anno.

Allo stato attuale, in funzione del grado di completamento delle opere dell’intero sistema idrico gestito dalla Società, le risorse utilizzate raggiungono mediamente valori inferiori a 600 milioni di metri cubi d’acqua all’anno. Le infrastrutture idrauliche di cui sopra sono suddivise in tre grandi schemi idrici ed un quarto, molto più limitato.

Origin Orbit our user friendly interface  containing a suite of satellite surveillance tools. Origin Orbit is of course excellent at locating water leaks on the potable water system. But can also be extremely effective in landslip monitoring, and therefore useful for a variety of infrastructure integrity purposes, including aqueducts, dams, and reservoirs.

Origin Find and Fix which uses a combination of satellite technology and our NoDig pipe repair product, to reduce water waste via leakage on the potable water network.

Name: Ministry of Irrigation and Lowlands (MILLs)
Description:
Irrigation has been used to develop large to medium-scale farms in Ethiopia. However, given the possibilities it offers for the country’s agriculture sector and overall economic development, it remains underutilized. Moreover, as Ethiopia has envisioned to become a middle-income country by 2023 E.C, the proper utilization of natural resources and irrigation technology can help in realizing this vision.
 
An important challenge that has to be solved for this vision to become a reality, is the question of food security and climate resilience. If used effectively, irrigation technology is an option that can help solve this issue and have positive trickle-down effects across other sectors. However, this question is also tied with the farmers, pastoralists and semi-pastoralists populations in lowland areas of the country. In the past, these regions of Ethiopia have been overlooked and not thoroughly explored, even though they hold potential opportunities. It is essential for irrigation development plans in these areas to be participatory and inclusive.

The Ministry of Irrigation and Lowlands (MILLs), was therefore established to leverage the vast land, water, animal and human resources found within the lowland areas of Ethiopia to help achieve the country in its development mission.

Name: Water150

Through a blockchain-secured ecosystem of verified spring wells, each W150 token grants the holder the right to receive 1 liter of premium-quality drinking water every year via an annual DROP voucher.

Naturally filtered water, sourced from audited wells across Northern Europe — with a global network of 1,000 springs underway.

All water sources are managed by the Longhouse Foundation and audited annually. Bottling is handled by Longhouse Water150 AB in Sweden, and token issuance by Longhouse Water150 S.A. in Luxembourg.

En este sentido, el objetivo fundamental de FENACORE es defender apolíticamente los intereses y derechos del agua, armonizando el esfuerzo y el trabajo de todas las partes involucradas en el regadío español y colaborando estrechamente con las distintas administraciones públicas en el diseño de la política del agua del país.
Este modelo de asociación de usuarios en Comunidades de Regantes, de gran experiencia en España, está siendo ampliamente divulgado por FENACORE en Europa del Este, Asia y África, con el objetivo de que sus recursos de agua puedan ser distribuidos con el máximo rigor y equidad.

It operates 46 water treatment plants (“WTPs”) statewide with a total treatment capacity of 2,133 million litres daily (“MLD”).

As at 31 December 2022, Ranhill SAJ’s network comprises 716 reservoirs and 24,002 KM of pipelines that supply treated water to 4 million people in Johor as well as industries across the state.

With a low NRW rating of 20.9 m³ / KM of Pipe / Day, the NRW percentage for Johor state is 26.3% in FY2022.

Working together with Ranhill Water Services Sdn Bhd (“RWS”), which specialises in NRW reduction and management, Ranhill SAJ has set a target of further reduction of Johor’s NRW levels to 20.9% by 2025.

At Ilion, we ambition to 𝗱𝗲𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗯𝗼𝗻𝗶𝘇𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗱𝗿𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝘀𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘄𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗶𝗻𝗱𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘆. 

Our technology, born from pioneering research at École Normale Supérieure (ENS) in Paris, transforms desalination by moving away from traditional high-pressure methods.

Instead, our proprieraty membrane-based electro-pump, leverages unconventional nanofluidic transport phenomena to drive water through reverse-osmosis filters, operating pressure-free.

This approach delivers a more energy-efficient, cost-effective, and safer solution, helping shape a sustainable future in water treatment.

Name: Biodynamizer
Description:
The 𝗕𝗶𝗼𝗱𝘆𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿 makes it possible to restore pure water, equivalent to mountain spring water, thanks to its hydrodynamic and magnetic technology. This innovative, award-winning, and scientifically validated system offers a two-step solution:
1. Biofilter (filtering)

Name: Jsub Irrigation - Sub irrigation emitters
Description:
Our patented subirrigation devices bring a whole new perspective to irrigation. In sub-irrigation systems, targeted root zone delivery is critical to maximizing water and nutrient efficiency. With our patented technology JSub and JStake emitters apply water and nutrients directly to the active root zone, minimizing evaporative losses, and surface run off. 

By operating below the soil surface the JSub or JStake significantly reduce weed germination, while improving soil moisture uniformity. The controlled delivery environment limits calcium bicarbonate accumulation associated with traditional irrigation, helping maintain soil structure and long-term infiltration rates. 

The result is improved nutrient uptake efficiency, reduced energy demand and enhanced soil health across a wide range of soil types and terrain conditions. All products are designed and manufactured in Woodlake, CA with durability and field scalability in mind. 

If interested, we invite you to join several growers who are currently participating in a FREE trial to see if our subirrigation technology is a fit for your operation.

Name: Aguatierra

Aguatierra: Because There Is No New Water

Aguatierra was born in Guatemala with a clear mission: to make clean water accessible to all through natural, plastic-free, and electricity-free ceramic filters.

 The Global Water Crisis
 365,000 children die each year from waterborne diseases.
 1.5 million people die annually from diarrheal diseases due to a lack of clean water and sanitation.
 46 billion plastic bottles are consumed in Europe every year.

 Our Solution
Our filters use a combination of ceramic, activated carbon, and colloidal silver to provide a natural and eco-friendly purification process.

 Independently tested to reduce pharmaceuticals to undetectable levels and remove over 99% of harmful PFAS, our natural, plastic-free solution provides exceptionally clean and safe drinking water for your home.

 Why Aguatierra?
 Fighting Plastic Pollution: With 46 billion plastic bottles consumed yearly in Europe, our plastic-free filters offer a sustainable and long-term alternative to bottled water.

 Saving Lives: Every year, 365,000 children under five die from waterborne diseases. By providing access to clean water, we help prevent these unnecessary deaths.

 A Simple Yet Powerful Solution: Our natural filtration technology ensures safe drinking water while reducing global reliance on plastic and mitigating the devastating effects of waterborne diseases.

 Join us in building a future where clean water is a right, not a privilege.

#BecauseThereIsNoNewWater #CleanWater #Aguatierra #Guatemala  #SustainableDevelopment #SocialImpact #ZeroPlastic #WaterAccess

Name: Persalt BR
Description:
Engenheiros com ampla experiência em tecnologias de separação de líquidos, adquirida desde a década de 1980, uniram-se para fundar a Persalt. Eles aceitaram o desafio de oferecer à indústria produtos de alta qualidade, com baixo custo operacional e mais acessíveis do que os fornecidos pelos líderes tradicionais do mercado.

Na Persalt, somos especializados em soluções avançadas para tratamento de água industrial e municipal, com foco em sustentabilidade e inovação.

Oferecemos membranas de osmose reversa e resinas de troca iônica de última geração para tratamento, reúso e processamento de água, maximizando o desempenho em sistemas de dessalinização e desmineralização.

Temos orgulho de nossa presença em expansão na América do Sul, onde conquistamos rapidamente uma fatia significativa do mercado, substituindo com eficiência produtos de concorrentes entre os principais usuários de membranas e resinas de troca iônica.

Esse sucesso se deve a dois fatores principais: nossa estrutura operacional otimizada, que nos permite oferecer produtos excepcionais com preços competitivos e a dedicação de nossos engenheiros especializados, focados em desenvolver soluções inovadoras para os desafios mais complexos no tratamento de água.

Todos os nossos produtos são certificados pela NSF e fabricados com materiais da mais alta qualidade.

Oferecemos membranas com características low fouling (LF) e baixa perda de carga (LPD), proporcionando menores custos operacionais e vida útil prolongada. Nossas membranas de ultrafiltração são produzidas com fibras robustas, ideais para aplicações em tratamento e reúso de água.

Além disso, nossa linha de resinas com tamanho uniforme de partícula inclui resinas de troca iônica de alta capacidade de troca e resinas adsorventes com ampla área superficial, o que contribui para a redução dos custos operacionais.

Oferecemos suporte técnico de excelência a todos os usuários de nossos produtos.

Aquaspot révolutionne l'équation : là où d'autres parlent encore, nous combinons technologie de pointe, engagement client et financement circulaire pour que chaque douche devienne un acte de transition où tous gagnent.

Name: Hydra Portugal
Description:
Na Hydra Portugal, sabemos que cuidar da água é cuidar de todos nós.

A água é essencial para a vida, e o modo como a tratamos reflete o nosso
compromisso com o futuro.

Estamos aqui para garantir que cada pessoa e empresa tenha acesso à melhor água possível, sem comprometer o planeta.
Desenvolvemos soluções que são tão eficazes quanto ecológicas, para que possa desfrutar de uma água excelente, sabendo que está a fazer a sua parte para um mundo mais sustentável.

Entendemos que as nossas escolhas de hoje moldam o bem-estar de amanhã.

É por isso que os nossos sistemas de tratamento de água respeitam tanto as necessidades humanas quanto os limites do nosso planeta.

Não se trata apenas de fornecer água pura; trata-se de honrar uma promessa para as gerações futuras.

A Hydra Portugal está na vanguarda de uma nova era no tratamento de água.

Combinamos inovação tecnológica com um profundo compromisso com a sustentabilidade para criar soluções que redefinem o conceito de qualidade.

Mais do que fornecer sistemas de tratamento, estamos a construir um legado de responsabilidade ambiental.

A nossa missão é assegurar que a pegada que deixamos no planeta seja tão limpa quanto a água que oferecemos.

Junte-se a nós nesta jornada para moldar um futuro onde a excelência e a sustentabilidade caminham de mãos dadas!

Description:
VILIVIA : une société dédiée à la gestion des systèmes d’assainissement sur le territoire de la Métropole Aix-Marseille-Provence
Depuis le 1er janvier 2025, VILIVIA assure l’exploitation des systèmes d’assainissement de 18 communes du Pays Salonnais, situées au nord-ouest de la Métropole Aix-Marseille-Provence, dans la continuité des missions précédemment assurées par Agglopole Provence Assainissement. 

Avec ce nouveau contrat, VILIVIA s’engage à garantir la continuité du service et à déployer de nombreux engagements techniques innovants, tout en renforçant ses actions en matière de sensibilisation du public et en intégrant des enjeux de responsabilité sociétale au cœur de son fonctionnement.

Issue d’un partenariat entre la Métropole et Saur, acteur engagé de l’eau et des services essentiels en France et à l’international, VILIVIA, forte de près de 40 collaborateurs mobilisés localement, et appuyée par les expertises techniques et les outils de Saur France, se positionne comme une société dédiée engagée pour un service d’assainissement collectif performant visant à préserver les milieux aquatiques sensibles du territoire, tout en limitant les nuisances pour les usagers.

Name: Δ.Ε.Υ.Α. ΔΗΜΟΥ Ι.Π. ΜΕΣΟΛΟΓΓΙΟΥ  (ΔΕΥΑΜ)
Description:
Η Δ.Ε.Υ.Α. Δήμου Ιερής Πόλης Μεσολογγίου ιδρύθηκε το 1993 (ΦΕΚ 65/1993, τ Α’) με την υπ’ αριθμ. 153/1992 απόφαση του Δημοτικού Συμβουλίου Ι.Π. Μεσολογγίου. Με βάση την τεχνικο-οικονομική έκθεση που συντάχθηκε από την ΕΕΤΑΑ τον Απρίλιο 1992. 

Βασικοί στόχοι της επιχείρησης είναι:

- Η προστασία των υδάτινων πόρων της περιοχής του Μεσολογγίου.
- Η προστασία του περιβάλλοντος και της θαλάσσιας περιοχής του Δήμου Μεσολογγίου.
- Ο εκσυγχρονισμός του τρόπου διαχείρισης του τομέα Ύδρευσης-Αποχέτευσης.
 

Αρχικά η περιοχή αρμοδιότητάς της περιορίζονταν στα όρια του τότε Δήμου Μεσολογγίου. Μετά την εφαρμογή του «Σχεδίου Καποδίστριας» το 1997, ήταν αρμόδια για την ύδρευση και αποχέτευση της συνολικής περιοχής του διευρυμένου πλέον Δήμου Μεσολογγίου (Μεσολόγγι, Ευηνοχώρι, Άγιος Γεώργιος, Άγιος Θωμάς, Ρέτσινα, Μούσουρα, Ελληνικά).

Με το νόμο 3852/2010 (πρόγραμμα Καλλικράτης) η ΔΕΥΑΜ επέκτεινε τα όρια αρμοδιότητάς της στους πρώην Δήμους Αιτωλικού και Οινιάδων (ΦΕΚ 802/2011 τ Β’).

24 hodin denně po 7 dnů v týdnu se mohou naši zákazníci a partneři spolehnout na skupinu ENERGIE AG BOHEMIA při dodávkách pitné vody, tepla i odvádění a čištění odpadních vod. 
 
Staráme se o celý koloběh vody. Počínaje vodou jako „surovinou“ odebíranou z vodárenských nádrží, pramenišť a vrtů, přes její úpravu na vodu pitnou až
po distribuci této vody jednomu miliónu obyvatel. Od nich přebíráme vodu odpadní a zajišťujeme, aby byla řádně vyčištěna na čistírnách odpadních vod a mohla se tak vrátit čistá do přirozeného koloběhu vody v přírodě. 

V domácnostech, školách a provozovnách našich zákazníků pečujeme o tepelný komfort a zajišťujeme dodávku teplé vody. Teplo vyrábíme v teplárnách na biomasu, centrálních i domovních plynových kotelnách, z nichž některé využívají vysoce účinné kogenerace tepla a elektrické energie.

Vodárenské a teplárenské služby v sobě zahrnují nesčetné provozní úkoly. Navíc mohou naši zákazníci využívat široké portfolio doprovodných služeb. Patří sem i stavebně-montážní činnost, projekce, laboratorní rozbory, smart-metering, speciální technika a nouzové vybavení a mnoho dalších. Naše služby však sahají až do samotných domácností. Vlastníkům a správcům domů nabízíme údržbu, opravy, odečítání měřidel, fakturaci a také správu bytového fondu.

S více než 1 800 zaměstnanci a obratem přes 4 miliardy korun jsme stabilním a profesionálním partnerem pro města a obce, svazky obcí a sdružení, průmyslové podniky, živnostníky, správce domů a koncové zákazníky v celé České republice.

Name: Partenariat National de l'Eau - PNE-Republique du Congo
Description:
Bienvenue sur la page officielle du Partenariat National de l'Eau (PNE) ! 

Le PNE est une initiative collaborative dédiée à la préservation et à la gestion durable des ressources en eau au niveau national. En tant que plateforme de coordination entre les différents acteurs du secteur de l'eau, notre mission est de favoriser la coopération, l'innovation et le partage de bonnes pratiques pour relever les défis liés à l'eau.

Nos objectifs principaux incluent :

 Promouvoir la sensibilisation à l'importance de l'eau en tant que ressource vitale et stratégique.
 Encourager la mise en œuvre de politiques et de programmes visant à assurer une gestion efficace et équitable de l'eau.
 Faciliter le dialogue entre les parties prenantes, y compris les gouvernements, les entreprises, les organisations de la société civile et les citoyens, afin de trouver des solutions durables aux problèmes de l'eau.
 Fournir des ressources, des outils et des informations pour soutenir les efforts de conservation et de protection des écosystèmes aquatiques.

Nous croyons fermement que la collaboration et l'engagement de tous sont essentiels pour garantir un avenir où l'eau est accessible à tous, préservée pour les générations futures et utilisée de manière équitable et durable.

Rejoignez-nous dans notre mission pour un avenir où l'eau est une source de vie et de prospérité pour tous !

Description:
Mohanpura Kundaliya Project Management Unit (MKPMU) is managing one of India's most innovative and impactful irrigation projects. With a demonstrated  enhancement water use efficiency and ensuring sustainable agricultural development, the project covers 2,90,000 hectares and benefits 1,300 villages and 400,000 farmers across the district making it one of the world's largest systems of its kind.

The project employs state of the art pressurized piped irrigation technology, increasing water use efficiency from 38% to 80%. By delivering water to the farmers’ fields ddirectly from the reservoir in a timely and efficient manner, the project reduces dependency on rainfall and increases productivity, enabling local communities to thrive.

Name: Amatola Water Board
Description:
Amatola Water is an essential water services utility in the Eastern Cape. 

Amatola Water’s primary activity, as stipulated under the provisions of Sections 29 and 30 of the Act, is to provide bulk water services.

A brief history of our organisation
In 1998, it was established as proclaimed in the Government Gazette No 18409 of 14 November 1997 by the Minister, as empowered by the Water Services Act (Act 108 of 1997) as well as the Public Finance Management Act No 1 of 1999 and is accountable to the Minister of Water and Sanitation as its executive authority. 

In 1998 we started providing water services primarily to municipalities, under the provisions of the Water Services Act (Act 108 of 1997). Sections 29 and 30 of the legislation state that the primary activity of a water board is to provide bulk water services, secondary activities being subject to such activities not affecting the water board´s primary role and capability.

Since its establishment in 2007, Amatola Water’s gazetted operational area has been grown more than three-fold to 47,515 km2.

Description:
The Metropolitan Waterworks and Sewerage System (MWSS), formerly known as the National Waterworks and Sewerage System Authority (NAWASA) is the government agency that is in charge of water privatization in Metro Manila in the Philippines.

On 19 June 1971, RA 6234 was enacted. It dissolved the NAWASA and created in its place the MWSS. MWSS was thus given the mandate “to ensure an uninterrupted and adequate supply and distribution of potable water for domestic and other purposes at just and equitable rates.”

With the power to simultaneously oxidise and disinfect, generating chlorine in-situ without the need for hazardous chemicals, our technology is reshaping the possibilities of water treatment.

Designed for both drinking water and wastewater, our system not only eradicates the need for costly and potentially harmful gas chlorination methods but also significantly reduces operational complexities and safety concerns. By eliminating the transport, storage, and handling of hazardous chemicals, we offer a sustainable and efficient alternative that's not only cost-effective but also minimises environmental impact.

Name: Methods for Irrigation and Agriculture (MIRRA)
Description:
MIRRA is a Jordanian, private, non-governmental, and not-for-profit research and development association established in 2007. 

OUR MISSION
MIRRA’s mission is to work toward the implementation of sustainable development initiatives in Jordan by addressing the challenges facing the water, agricultural, and environmental sectors in order to contribute to water conservation, environmental sustainability, and social and economic prosperity.

IMPACT
Our association’s initiatives aim to have a direct impact on achieving SDG 6 – Access to water and sanitation for all; SDG 13 – Urgent action to combat climate change and its impacts.; and SDG 17 – Strengthen the means of implementation and revitalize the global partnership for sustainable development. MIRRA also takes into great consideration the following SDGs through its projects and intervention implementation: SDG 4 – Quality education; SDG 5 – Gender equality; and SDG 7 – Affordable and clean energy.

OUR APPROACH IS CENTERD AROUND FIVE PRINCIPLES

1- INNOVATION: MIRRA is dedicated to finding and implementing innovative and creative solutions. Since 2007, we have worked with researchers and industrial private sector in Europe and the USA to pilot, demonstrate and transfer technology.

2- SUSTAINABILITY: We focus on elements of durability and continuity in all our interventions, which contributes to their long-lasting benefits.

3- ADABTABILITY: We employ technologies and approaches that are validated specifically to local field conditions and community contexts.

4- STAKEHOLDERS INVOLVEMENT: All stakeholders are involved every step of the way in our development programs in order to ensure the highest level of participation, education and capacity-building.

5- NETWORKING: Communication and collaboration with local, regional and international institutions are key factors in our successful implementation of programs.
